annotations.label: Mathematical constant

anchor.snippet: A mathematical constant is a number whose value is fixed by an unambiguous definition

annotations.note: An informal meta-mathematical notion with no corresponding Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Named constants are definable

anchor.snippet: All named mathematical constants are definable numbers

annotations.note: An informal claim about definability of constants; not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square root of 2

anchor.snippet: the unique positive real number that, when multiplied by itself, gives the number 2

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Real.sqrt 2` with `Real.sq_sqrt`/`Real.sqrt_sq` characterizing it as the unique nonnegative real squaring to 2.

annotations.label: √2 as diagonal of unit square

anchor.snippet: Geometrically the square root of 2 is the length of a diagonal across a square with sides of one unit of length

annotations.note: No specific geometric statement identifying √2 with the diagonal of a unit square is recorded in Mathlib.

annotations.label: √2 is irrational and algebraic

anchor.snippet: It is an irrational number, possibly the first number to be known as such, and an algebraic number

annotations.note: Irrationality is `irrational_sqrt_two`; algebraicity of √2 over ℚ has no dedicated lemma but follows trivially from `Real.sq_sqrt`.

annotations.label: 99/70 approximation of √2

anchor.snippet: the quick approximation 99/70 (≈ 1.41429) for the square root of two was frequently used before the common use of electronic calculators

annotations.note: No Mathlib lemma records the specific rational approximation 99/70 for √2.

annotations.label: Definition of π

anchor.snippet: The constant π (pi) has a natural definition in Euclidean geometry as the ratio between the circumference and diameter of a circle

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Real.pi` analytically via the first positive zero of cos rather than geometrically, but the resulting constant is the same.

annotations.label: Hydrogen ground state wave function

anchor.snippet: the ground state wave function of the hydrogen atom

annotations.note: Quantum-mechanical wave functions for the hydrogen atom are not part of Mathlib.

annotations.label: π is irrational, transcendental, a period

anchor.snippet: π is an irrational number , transcendental number and an algebraic period

annotations.note: Irrationality of π is `irrational_pi`; transcendence of π and its status as a period are not formalized.

annotations.label: 22/7 and 355/113 approximations of π

anchor.snippet: Unusually good approximations are given by the fractions 22/7 and 355/113

annotations.note: Mathlib does not record the rational approximations 22/7 or 355/113 for π.

annotations.label: Definition of e

anchor.snippet: one possible definition of it is the value of the following expression

annotations.note: Euler's number is `Real.exp 1`; the limit definition `(1+1/n)^n → e` is the special case of `tendsto_one_add_div_pow_exp` at `t = 1`.

annotations.label: e and the exponential function

anchor.snippet: The constant e is intrinsically related to the exponential function

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Real.exp` is the real exponential function with `Real.exp 1` exactly Euler's number.

annotations.label: Bernoulli's compound interest

anchor.snippet: Jacob Bernoulli discovered that e arises in compound interest

annotations.note: The underlying limit `(1 + t/n)^n → exp t` is formalized; the financial interpretation is not.

annotations.label: Slot machine probability

anchor.snippet: suppose that a slot machine with a one in n probability of winning is played n times

annotations.note: The slot-machine limit `(1 - 1/n)^n → 1/e` is the `t = -1` specialization of `tendsto_one_add_div_pow_exp`, but no probabilistic packaging is given.

annotations.label: Derangements / hat check problem

anchor.snippet: is in the problem of derangements , also known as the hat check problem

annotations.note: Mathlib proves `numDerangements n / n! → exp (-1)`.

annotations.label: e is irrational and transcendental

anchor.snippet: e is an irrational number and a transcendental number

annotations.note: Neither irrationality nor transcendence of `Real.exp 1` is recorded under any obvious name in Mathlib (`irrational_e` and `transcendental_e` do not exist).

annotations.label: Imaginary unit

anchor.snippet: is a mathematical concept which extends the real number system to the complex number system

annotations.note: `Complex.I : ℂ` is the imaginary unit.

annotations.label: i² = −1

anchor.snippet: The imaginary unit's core property is that i 2 = −1

annotations.note: Stated literally as `Complex.I_sq : I ^ 2 = -1`.

annotations.label: Two complex square roots of −1

anchor.snippet: There are in fact two complex square roots of −1, namely i and − i

annotations.note: No lemma `x ^ 2 = -1 ↔ x = I ∨ x = -I` (or equivalent) appears in Mathlib's complex library.

annotations.label: Diagonal of regular pentagon equals φ times its side

anchor.snippet: the length of a regular pentagon 's diagonal is φ times its side

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the regular pentagon nor its diagonal-to-side ratio.

annotations.label: Icosahedron vertices from golden rectangles

anchor.snippet: The vertices of a regular icosahedron are those of three mutually orthogonal golden rectangles

annotations.note: Mathlib has no construction of the regular icosahedron or a description of its vertices via golden rectangles.

annotations.label: Kepler: φ as limit of Fibonacci ratios

anchor.snippet: Kepler proved that it is the limit of the ratio of consecutive Fibonacci numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib proves `fib (n+1) / fib n → φ` as `n → ∞`.

annotations.label: Slowest converging continued fraction

anchor.snippet: The golden ratio has the slowest converging continued fraction of any irrational number

annotations.note: Mathlib does not state any optimality claim about φ's continued fraction convergence rate.

annotations.label: Euler–Mascheroni constant

anchor.snippet: Euler's constant or the Euler–Mascheroni constant is defined as the limiting difference between the harmonic series and the natural logarithm

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Real.eulerMascheroniConstant` as the limit `H_n - log n`.

annotations.label: Apéry's constant

anchor.snippet: Apery's constant is defined as the sum of the reciprocals of the cubes of the natural numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has `riemannZeta` and so `riemannZeta 3` exists, but no dedicated `apery` constant or `tsum (1/n^3)` identification is recorded.

annotations.label: Euler's solution to the Basel problem

anchor.snippet: Euler famously solved the Basel problem by giving

annotations.note: `hasSum_zeta_two` states `∑ 1/n^2 = π²/6`.

annotations.label: Apéry: ζ(3) is irrational

anchor.snippet: is known to be an irrational number which was proven by the French mathematician Roger Apéry in 1979

annotations.note: Apéry's theorem on the irrationality of ζ(3) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Catalan's constant

anchor.snippet: Catalan's constant is defined by the alternating sum of the reciprocals of the odd square numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has Catalan numbers but no definition of Catalan's analytic constant G.

annotations.label: Feigenbaum constants α and δ

anchor.section: The Feigenbaum constants α and δ
anchor.snippet: the constant α is the ratio between the width of a tine and the width of one of its two subtines, and the constant δ is the limiting ratio of each bifurcation interval to the next

annotations.note: Feigenbaum's bifurcation constants are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Liouville and Champernowne constants

anchor.section: Simple representatives of sets of numbers
anchor.snippet: Some constants, such as the square root of 2 , Liouville's constant and Champernowne constant

annotations.note: `liouvilleNumber` is defined; the Champernowne constant is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hippasus: irrationality of √2

anchor.section: Simple representatives of sets of numbers
anchor.snippet: the Pythagorean Hippasus of Metapontum who proved, most likely geometrically, the irrationality of the square root of 2

annotations.note: The mathematical content (irrationality of √2) is `irrational_sqrt_two`.

annotations.label: Liouville's constant is transcendental

anchor.section: Simple representatives of sets of numbers
anchor.snippet: it was the first number to be proven transcendental

annotations.note: Mathlib proves `Transcendental ℤ (liouvilleNumber m)` for `2 ≤ m`.

annotations.label: Chaitin's constant

anchor.snippet: Chaitin's constant is the real number representing the probability that a randomly chosen Turing machine will halt

annotations.note: Chaitin's halting probability constant Ω is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chaitin's constant is transcendental and normal

anchor.snippet: has been proven to be transcendental and normal

annotations.note: No transcendence or normality result for Chaitin's Ω appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 0.999... equals 1

anchor.snippet: the two representations 0.999... and 1 are equivalent

annotations.note: `ofDigits_const_last_eq_one` proves the base-b identity `0.(b-1)(b-1)… = 1`, which specializes at `b = 10` to `0.999… = 1`.
